sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
The cortex ontology is defined in `cortex_ontology.yaml` at the project root. It specifies all valid node types, edge types, and validation rules. The builder enforces these rules at build time — invalid edges fail the build.

The ontology is based on PROV-O (W3C Provenance Ontology) principles with a process-centric model: all code is a series of processes, and the node type describes the process role, not the language construct.

sections.heading: Node Types
sections.content:
Three structural node types (ADR-009: reduced from five. `document` and `constraint` retired.)

### atomic_process
A leaf-level transformation unit with no sub-processes. The smallest meaningful unit of work.
- Subtypes: `function`, `docjson`, `test`
- Examples: Python function, dFlow `@task`, DocJSON section, test function
- `function` — Python function or method
- `docjson` — DocJSON doc section (code_hash = prose body, desc_hash = heading)
- `test` — Test function (identified by `test_` prefix or test file path)

### composite_process
Orchestrates or contains other processes. Scale-invariant: a module, package, pipeline, and `@workflow` are all composite_process.
- Subtypes: `module`, `docjson`, `external_package`
- Examples: Python module, package, dFlow `@workflow`, DocJSON doc file
- `module` — Python module or package
- `docjson` — DocJSON doc file (contains sections via `composes` edges)
- `external_package` — Third-party dependency

### entity
Something consumed or produced by a process.
- Subtypes: `data_artifact`, `config_artifact`, `model_artifact`
- Examples: AnnData object, config.yaml, trained model
- Always CLEAN in staleness checks (data artifacts, not logic)

sections.heading: Edge Types
sections.content:
| Edge Type | From Types | To Types | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `consumes` | atomic_process, composite_process | entity | Process takes entity as input |
| `produces` | atomic_process, composite_process | entity | Process creates entity as output |
| `composes` | composite_process | atomic_process, composite_process | Structural containment (module→function, doc file→section) |
| `delegates_to` | composite_process | atomic_process, composite_process | Runtime invocation (cross-module call) |
| `depends_on` | atomic_process, composite_process | atomic_process, composite_process, entity | Execution/import dependency |
| `constrains` | atomic_process | atomic_process, composite_process, entity | Schema/contract restriction (ADR-009: was `constraint →`) |
| `validates` | atomic_process | atomic_process, composite_process | Test coverage link (ADR-009: `constraint` dropped from from-types) |
| `documents` | atomic_process, composite_process | any | Documentation describes a node (ADR-009: was `document →`) |
| `supersedes` | atomic_process, composite_process | atomic_process, composite_process | Replacement/obsolescence (ADR-009: `constraint` dropped) |

sections.heading: Validation Rules
sections.content:
Enforced by `cortex/index/builder.py` at build time:

| Rule | Level | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `require_valid_node_type` | Error | Every node must have a node_type from the ontology |
| `require_valid_edge_type` | Error | Every edge must have an edge_type from the ontology |
| `require_valid_edge_endpoints` | Error | Edge from/to node types must match the edge_type's allowed types |
| `warn_empty_composite` | Warning | composite_process with no outgoing composes/delegates_to edges |
| `report_untested_atomic` | Info | atomic_process with no inbound validates edge (untested function) |

Edge validation is also enforced at runtime by `cortex link` via `cortex/ontology.py::validate_edge()`.
